explicit_text: I am Sandy Waugh Bradshaw, originally from Climax, North Carolina, and I worked as a flight attendant for United Airlines. By the time of the September 11 attacks, I had been flying for eleven years but was only doing two trips a month to spend more time with my family, including my two toddlers, teenage stepdaughter, and my husband who is a pilot for US Airways. On that tragic day, I was aboard United Airlines Flight 93 and took immediate action by calling United Airlines to report the hijacking and describing the terrorists. I also managed to call my husband at our home in Greensboro, North Carolina, to update him on the situation, including how we were boiling water to defend ourselves. It was a terrifying experience, but I did my best to help protect the passengers and crew.

explicit_text: The United States National Park Service maintains the official memorial and information about Flight 93 and its crew, including myself, Sandy Waugh Bradshaw. explicit_text: The September 11 attacks were a defining moment in my life and career as a flight attendant on United Airlines Flight 93. On that day, terrorists hijacked our plane, and I was involved in reporting the situation to United Airlines and communicating with my husband at home in Greensboro, North Carolina. We tried to defend ourselves by boiling water to throw on the hijackers, and I witnessed passengers moving toward first class to confront the threat. The events of that day tested all of us, and I am proud of the courage shown by everyone on board.

explicit_text: I was a flight attendant on United Airlines Flight 93 on September 11, 2001, when the plane was hijacked by terrorists. During the ordeal, I called United Airlines to report the emergency and described the hijackers, and I also called my husband at our home in Greensboro, North Carolina, to keep him informed. We were boiling water to defend ourselves, and passengers were moving toward first class to try to regain control of the plane. Flight 93 was a terrifying and heroic experience that I will never forget.
